given: Two verified immutable release directories and an isolated installation root
when: The release lifecycle advances the current link and retains its predecessor
acceptance: Installation switches an immutable current release

A double-click Windows installation asks the user to choose one absolute Personal Agent root and keeps immutable Core, mutable Workspace data, installation staging, upgrades, rollback and uninstall state under that selected root; explicit CLI installation remains non-interactive and macOS behavior is unchanged
Fresh installation opens the local Setup Center directly in the Tauri desktop shell without desktop login and presents WeChat as an optional connection without requiring a development Agent

The desktop silently starts managed Cloud enrollment, Sites domain allocation and Agent mail identity synchronization; only a failed or cancelled silent attempt exposes manual recovery and explicit disconnect suppresses automatic reconnection

Sites and local mail expose a keyboard-accessible custom-domain choice beside the platform-domain action; the three-step self-hosted flow accepts only a domain in an approved R2 plan, guides the user to prepare a keyed outbound WSS Relay without WireGuard or local administrator rights and configure DNS, maps the apex and Space subdomains across the installation, and commits only after final HTTPS content or real local-mail evidence

The desktop shell uses the system WebView, exposes no native page API, starts and continuously monitors the local runtime while open, restarts it after repeated health failures, stops it when closed, and remains bound to current/previous rollback
The local Console and main Agent share one digest-bound update plan, verified platform artifact, native desktop handoff, and persisted restart result

A pre-release self-contained candidate can create a ten-minute digest-bound plan only when its commit, manifest, checksums, SBOM, candidate security metadata and platform agree; it reuses the desktop handoff and immutable installer, records candidateAssetRuntime=true, and never satisfies public Release acceptance

Applying executable updates normally supports authenticated local approval; a registered Owner-initiated product-development flow authorizes the exact digest-bound plan by policy without a second local confirmation

The managed Cloud reverse tunnel exposes only the password-protected mobile surface; a self-hosted custom-domain Relay delegates full authenticated routes to the local gateway without platform quotas, and password reset invalidates existing remote sessions
Browser and CLI recovery remain available
Platform releases bundle the pinned OpenCLI production graph without lifecycle scripts or customer-side npm installation, while Chrome retains explicit Browser Bridge permission
